title: epipremnum carolinense
intro: Epipremnum carolinense, also known as the Polynesian ivy or Centipede Tongavine, is a tropical plant native to the Pacific Islands and belongs to the Arum family. The plant's leaves and roots have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Epipremnum carolinense include reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and relieving respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant.
